title: `podman rm -f` doesn't remove container when exec on container is in use.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
`podman rm -f` doesn't remove container when exec on container is in use.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create container

2. run podman exec -it /bin/bash on it 

3. run podman rm -f <container>

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ podman --log-level=debug rm -f 6259dd048707
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called rm.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level=debug rm -f 6259dd048707) 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"              
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/home/jaydoubleu/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/home/jaydoubleu/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000/containers     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/home/jaydoubleu/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/home/jaydoubleu/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that metacopy is not being used 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that native-diff is usable 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=btrfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=true, usingMetacopy=false 
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] configured OCI runtime runc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"            
INFO[0000] Found CNI network podman (type=bridge) at /var/home/jaydoubleu/.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist 
DEBU[0000] Default CNI network name podman is unchangeable 
INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 49             
DEBU[0000] Starting parallel job on container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e 
DEBU[0000] Removing container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e 
DEBU[0000] Removing all exec sessions for container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e 
DEBU[0000] Going to stop container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e exec session c461e9b5ed103c9ed7b7089dc4178478d58cf83c397ffa9ce1dc0c18f17d5ed8 
DEBU[0000] Killing exec session c461e9b5ed103c9ed7b7089dc4178478d58cf83c397ffa9ce1dc0c18f17d5ed8 (PID 13901) of container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e with SIGTERM 
INFO[0010] Timed out waiting for container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e exec session c461e9b5ed103c9ed7b7089dc4178478d58cf83c397ffa9ce1dc0c18f17d5ed8 to stop, resorting to SIGKILL: given PIDs did not die within timeout 
DEBU[0010] Killing exec session c461e9b5ed103c9ed7b7089dc4178478d58cf83c397ffa9ce1dc0c18f17d5ed8 (PID 13901) of container 6259dd048707b223030a8748a1b4be9f8d8256bb1c5243e8d45c1c94f6d6e96e with SIGKILL 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
podman should forcefully remove container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 21:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

It does seem to affect containers created with toolbx mainly.
running `watch date` within toolbox container while running podman rm -f on it's container will kill the watch process but not bash.

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out! I am unable to reproduce, unfortunately.

I'm able to reproduce on toolbx containers.
1., `toolbox create`
2. `toolbox enter`
3. `podman rm -f <toolbox container id>` ( from separate terminal )

I'll try to create some podman containers with flags used by toolbx (pid=host etc) to see if I can reproduce there as well.

Are the logs you provided complete? Does Podman just hang after that, or does it keep going?

It seems like an exec session process is ignoring SIGKILL, which means it's probably a zombie?

Thanks correct. It just hangs there doing nothing. When I exit the toolbox container it removes it straight away

```
DEBU[0000] Killing exec session 811669df013ed89bb0245e353d8af3cf914cc4ba14d4c5975334656a81e7aa66 (PID 11669) of container d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91 with SIGTERM 
INFO[0010] Timed out waiting for container d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91 exec session 811669df013ed89bb0245e353d8af3cf914cc4ba14d4c5975334656a81e7aa66 to stop, resorting to SIGKILL: given PIDs did not die within timeout 
DEBU[0010] Killing exec session 811669df013ed89bb0245e353d8af3cf914cc4ba14d4c5975334656a81e7aa66 (PID 11669) of container d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91 with SIGKILL 
```

Right after I exit the toolbox.
```
DEBU[0033] Cleaning up container d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91 
DEBU[0033] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...   
DEBU[0034] Successfully cleaned up container d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91 
DEBU[0034] unmounted container "d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91" 
DEBU[0034] Container d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91 storage is already unmounted, skipping... 
d2d73ebee332db09c242c0d004cfe003122d319a5e19b1a5660cefe9d8da9b91
DEBU[0034] Called rm.PersistentPostRunE(podman --log-level=debug rm -f d2d73ebee332) 
```

Toolbox containers aren't `--pid=host` last I checked, so it does make sense, killing the entire container forces the kernel to unwind the PID namespace and remove all processes in it, which forces the termination of the hung processes.

We shouldn't be hanging on exec sessions that fail to die, that does seem like a bug.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I can not repeat this with podman 4.0,  I tried both with --pid=host and without.  Both exited cleanly. I am going to assume this is fixed,  If not then we need a reproducer with podman commands, not just toolbox.
